question: Should the Kanban board allow drag-and-drop to move feature cards between phase columns (which would change a feature's current phase), or is the board read-only for MVP?
options: Read-only for MVP — cards display current phase, clicking navigates to detail page only
Drag-and-drop to move cards between phase columns, which updates the feature's current_phase via the API
Drag-and-drop with confirmation prompt before changing phase
Other

question: How should the user's preferred view (list vs kanban) be persisted across page reloads?
options: URL query parameter (?view=kanban) so it is shareable and bookmarkable
Browser localStorage so it persists per-browser without affecting URLs
Both — URL query param takes precedence, fall back to localStorage default
In-memory only (resets to list view on each reload)
Other

question: How should empty phase columns (phases with zero features) be displayed on the Kanban board?
options: Always show all six phase columns, with an empty-state message inside empty columns
Hide columns that have no features
Always show all six columns, empty columns rendered as a slim placeholder (no empty-state message)
Other

question: Which features should appear on the Kanban board? Features currently have statuses: draft, in_progress, gate_blocked, passed, failed, done, cancelled, recirculated, waiting_for_human.
options: Show all non-cancelled features, placed in the column matching their current_phase (including done/delivered features in the Delivery column)
Show all features except cancelled and fully-delivered (done) ones
Show only features currently in an active pipeline state (draft, in_progress, gate_blocked, recirculated, waiting_for_human, passed, failed) — hide done and cancelled
Other

question: Should each phase column display a count badge showing the number of feature cards in that column?
options: Yes — show a count badge in each column header (e.g., 'Inception (3)')
No — column headers show only the phase label
Other

question: Should the Kanban board support filtering features by priority within the board view for MVP (e.g., show only P1 cards)?
options: No filtering for MVP — show all features in the board; filtering is a later enhancement
Yes — a priority filter dropdown (All / P1 / P2 / P3) that hides non-matching cards across all columns
Yes — priority filter plus a status filter (active / blocked / done)
Other
